controller_plus_0xA0.best_current_name: tertiary auth request string or six-character auth code string
controller_plus_0xA0.writer: 0x14015455B..0x140154592 copies request-prep stack argument 6 into controller +0xA0 through helper 0x140031B10.
controller_plus_0xA0.consumer: 0x140154CA0 measures this string-like object and, when the length is exactly six, parses it with 0x1407C01C0 base 10 and writes a compact three-byte payload.
controller_plus_0xA0.constructor_default: 0x140151CA1 writes word 0x0117 at controller +0xB7, leaving the +0xA0 small-string sentinel byte at +0xB7 set to 0x17.

controller_plus_0xB8.writer: 0x14015459F..0x1401545A7 loads request-prep stack argument 7 from [RSP+0x80] and stores AL into controller +0xB8.
controller_plus_0xB8.consumer: 0x140154CD5..0x140154CE7 only reads +0xB8 when len(controller +0xA0) == 6, choosing selector 1 when nonzero and selector 3 when zero.
controller_plus_0xB8.constructor_default: 0x140151CA1 writes word 0x0117 at controller +0xB7, which also initializes the following byte controller +0xB8 to 1.
controller_plus_0xB8.important_negative_result: The totp-map lookup path that writes controller +0xB9/+0xBC does not write +0xB8. +0xB8 belongs to request setup and only affects the six-character +0xA0 code path.

relevant_argument_mapping.evidence: 0x14015455B loads the argument, 0x140154560 takes controller +0xA0, and 0x140154592 calls 0x140031B10 to copy the string-like object.
0x14015459F loads the byte and 0x1401545A7 stores it to controller +0xB8.

packet_selector_rules_after_this_milesto.payload: parse +0xA0 as a base-10 value through 0x1407C01C0 and write three bytes
parse +0xA0 as a base-10 value through 0x1407C01C0 and write three bytes
write the cached controller +0xBC dword from the totp-map lookup path
advance the packet cursor by four bytes in this helper

callsite_observations.impact: This path forces selector 3 whenever the installed +0xA0 string is six characters.
This path also forces selector 3 for a six-character +0xA0 string.

callsite_observations.plus_0xB8_source: caller-provided byte or wrapper-local byte propagated through AL/DIL/SIL/BPL/RDI/RSI rather than a value derived by the packet helper
callsite_observations.impact: Most request-prep wrappers decide +0xB8 before the packet selector helper runs; the packet helper only consumes the already-staged mode byte.

opennxt_947_impact: The 947 auth/TOTP extension should model the six-character-code branch as selector 1 or selector 3 plus a compact three-byte numeric payload. The selector is controlled by a setup-time mode byte, not by the code string itself.
The cached-dword TOTP branch remains separate: it depends on +0xB9/+0xBC from the totp map and is only used when the staged +0xA0 string is not exactly six characters.
Server compatibility tests should cover all four selector cases: 0 for cached dword, 1 and 3 for six-character code payloads, and 2 for the reserved or skipped four-byte path.
next_targets: Resolve the dynamic +0xB8 wrapper inputs that are not immediate zero, especially the AL/DIL/SIL/BPL propagation sites in the 0x1401530C0, 0x1401531E9, 0x140153A16, 0x140153C5F, 0x14015420B, 0x1401542ED, and 0x1401F5731 callers.
Name the concrete auth/UI action that enters the forced selector-3 path at 0x140153E70.
Correlate selector values 1 and 3 with OpenNXT 947 login/auth packet decoder behavior.
Continue mapping the upstream stream dword that becomes controller +0xBC for selector 0.
